recentItems.title: 2026 Multi-country Andes Strain Hantavirus Outbreak Associated with a Cruise Ship - KDHE (.gov)
recentItems.summary: A multi-country outbreak of Andes strain hantavirus linked to the cruise ship MV Hondius has resulted in 12 confirmed cases and 5 deaths across four countries; the Kansas Department of Health and Environment stated that 248 passengers and crew remain in quarantine.

recentItems.summary: The 2026 MV Hondius hantavirus outbreak resulted in 72 confirmed cases and 6 deaths. The ship docked in the Netherlands for disinfection, with 8 individuals in quarantine.

recentItems.title: These are the U.S. states monitoring for hantavirus cases linked to cruise ship at center of outbreak - Yahoo
recentItems.summary: As of early 2026, 8 U.S. states—including Florida, Texas, and New York—are monitoring passengers from the MV Hondius cruise ship for hantavirus infections, following an outbreak with 27 confirmed cases and 6 deaths. Health officials have placed 312 passengers and crew under quarantine, with the CDC stating they are "working closely with state partners to track all potential exposures."
